channel.title: AI·Brief — Daily Tech Intelligence
channel.description: Automated AI-powered tech news briefings updated every 3 hours.

item.title: Apple Approves Poke as First AI Agent on Messages
item.description: Poke is the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform, simplifying AI interactions via text. This marks a significant milestone for AI integration in messaging.

item.title: PS5 vs Pizza: God of War Sparks Social Media Spat
item.description: A playful exchange between Domino's and Sony over God of War has ignited a social media spat, showcasing the intersection of gaming and marketing. Fans are enjoying the lighthearted banter.

item.title: No Rest For The Wicked Xbox Port Delayed
item.description: The Xbox and Switch ports of No Rest for the Wicked are delayed due to memory issues with the Series S, while the PS5 version launches as planned. This raises concerns about platform parity.

item.title: WWDC 2026: Siri Revamp and Apple Intelligence Updates
item.description: Anticipation builds for WWDC 2026, featuring a major Siri overhaul and updates to Apple Intelligence. Developers are eager to see how these changes will enhance user experience.

item.title: 2027 Dodge Charger Daytona Price Hike Explained
item.description: The 2027 Dodge Charger Daytona sees a $12,500 price increase without major updates, raising eyebrows among car enthusiasts. This unusual pricing strategy has sparked discussions.

item.title: Belkin’s Joy-Con Grips Boost Switch 2 Battery Life
item.description: Belkin introduces a new Charging Grip for the Switch 2, featuring a 10,000mAh battery that enhances gameplay and comfort. This accessory aims to improve the gaming experience significantly.

item.title: Atlus Acknowledges Next Persona Game After Leak
item.description: A recent leak has prompted Atlus to confirm the development of the next Persona game, exciting fans eager for updates. This acknowledgment hints at upcoming announcements.

item.title: Xbox Demo Fest Now Live with 30+ Free Games
item.description: The Xbox Demo Fest 2026 has launched, offering players access to over 40 games for free until June 30. This event encourages gamers to explore new titles without cost.

item.title: Famitsu Sales Data for Japan: May 25-31, 2026
item.description: Famitsu reports estimated physical game sales in Japan for the week of May 25-31, 2026, highlighting top-selling titles. This data provides insights into gaming trends in the region.

item.title: Microsoft's Linux Push Continues at Build 2026
item.description: Microsoft is enhancing its Linux offerings with new server and container versions, alongside Windows 11 tailored for Linux developers. This marks a significant shift in the company's strategy.

item.title: PS5 Gets Major RPG Exclusive, Fans Upset
item.description: Control Resonant, a major RPG, will be exclusively available early on PS5, causing backlash from players on other platforms. This exclusivity raises concerns about fairness in gaming.

item.title: Oura Ring 5: A Game Changer in Wearables
item.description: The Oura Ring 5 impresses with its sleek design and advanced health tracking features, setting a new standard in wearables. Users are excited about its potential benefits.

item.title: MGM and Amazon to Publish Future James Bond Games
item.description: MGM and Amazon Game Studios will publish future IO Interactive James Bond titles following Amazon's acquisition of MGM. This partnership aims to enhance the franchise's reach.

item.title: Crystal Dynamics on AI Use in Tomb Raider
item.description: Crystal Dynamics discusses its use of AI in Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis, aiming to empower creativity and flexibility within its teams. This approach reflects industry trends.

item.title: Microsoft to Customize Windows 11 Right-Click Menu
item.description: Microsoft will allow users to customize the right-click menu in Windows 11, addressing long-standing complaints about its clutter. This change aims to enhance user experience.
